source: Basic Conditions of Employment Act 75 of 1997 - Sections 20, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27

annual_leave.accrual_method: 1 day for every 17 days worked, OR 1.25 working days per month
annual_leave.grant_timing: Must be granted within 6 months after end of annual leave cycle
annual_leave.annual_leave_cycle: 12 months from start of employment or anniversary
annual_leave.pay_in_lieu_prohibition: Employer may not pay employee instead of granting leave, except on termination
annual_leave.carry_over: By agreement, unused leave may be carried over but must be used within 6 months
annual_leave.payment_on_termination: Pro-rated accrued leave must be paid out on termination
annual_leave.public_holidays_excluded: Public holidays falling within annual leave do not count as leave days

sick_leave.working_days_equivalent: Equivalent to the number of days the employee would normally work in a 6-week period

first_6_months.description: During first 6 months of employment: 1 day for every 26 days worked

sick_leave.medical_certificate_required_if: Absent for more than 2 consecutive days, or absent more than twice in 8 weeks
sick_leave.notes: Sick leave cycle runs for 36 months from start of employment. At start of new cycle, balance resets to 30 days.

qualifying_events: Death of employee's spouse or life partner
Death of employee's parent, adoptive parent, grandparent, child, adopted child, grandchild, or sibling
family_responsibility_leave.unused_leave: Lapses at end of annual leave cycle; does not carry over

maternity_leave.latest_start: Date of birth if not already on leave
maternity_leave.unpaid_by_employer: BCEA does not require employer to pay during maternity leave; employee claims from UIF
maternity_leave.dismissal_protection: Employer may not dismiss during or on account of maternity leave

parental_leave.applies_to: Any parent (not on maternity leave) on birth of child or adoption/commissioning parent

adoption_leave.applies_to: Adoptive parent of child under age 2
